title: rubus scaberrimus var. gracilis
intro: Rubus scaberrimus var. gracilis, also known as the dwarf or cane-rose, is a variety of the bramble plant that belongs to the rose family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Rubus scaberrimus var. gracilis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in the production of jams and preserves, although it's worth noting that some sources report the plant as potentially toxic.
